name: pr_review
description: Unattended PR review pipeline: audits the diff for security and logic issues, converges the findings, applies fixes, runs the test suite, and merges once green. If the test suite still fails after a fix pass, loops back to the fixer (capped) instead of asking a human.

variables.description: The PR identifier to review, e.g. from a run request like 'run pr review for PR #482'.
Security issues found in the diff (injection, auth/authz, secrets, unsafe deserialization, SSRF, path traversal, vulnerable dependencies, etc.), one per line with file, line, severity, and the issue.

Logic/correctness issues found in the same diff (off-by-one errors, incorrect conditionals, missing edge cases, race conditions, broken control flow, incorrect state mutations, etc.), one per line with file, line, severity, and the issue.
security_findings and logic_findings merged into one deduplicated, severity-ordered list of actionable issues.
Whether converged_findings contains at least one actionable issue.
Number of fix/test cycles run so far in this review.
Whether the test suite passed on the most recent run.
Which tests failed and why, from the most recent test run; guides the next fix pass.

settings.action: Fetch the full diff for pr_number (e.g. `gh pr diff <pr_number>`). Keep the diff content available for the audit steps that follow.

Audit the diff for security vulnerabilities: injection, authentication/authorization gaps, hardcoded secrets, unsafe deserialization, SSRF, path traversal, insecure use of dependencies, and similar OWASP-class issues. Write each finding to security_findings with the file, line, severity, and a description of the issue. If nothing is found, set security_findings to empty.

Audit the same diff for logic and correctness issues: off-by-one errors, incorrect conditionals, missing or mishandled edge cases, race conditions, broken control flow, and incorrect state mutations. Write each finding to logic_findings with the file, line, severity, and a description of the issue. If nothing is found, set logic_findings to empty.

Merge security_findings and logic_findings into converged_findings: remove duplicates and issues that are not actually actionable, and order what remains by severity (highest first). Set findings_present to true if converged_findings has at least one actionable issue, otherwise set it to false.
Initialize fix_iteration to 0, tests_passed to false, and test_output to empty for a fresh fix/test cycle.

Increment fix_iteration by 1. On the first pass, fix every issue listed in converged_findings with the minimal correct change for each. On a later pass (test_output is non-empty), instead fix whatever test_output says is failing. Do not make unrelated changes.

Run the project's test suite. If every test passes, set tests_passed to true and clear test_output. Otherwise set tests_passed to false and write which tests failed and why into test_output, so the next fix pass knows exactly what to address.
The test suite passes. Merge pr_number (e.g. `gh pr merge <pr_number> --squash`), set merged to true, and post a summary of the converged findings that were fixed before merging.

The fix/test cycle cap (3 iterations) was reached without the test suite passing. Do not merge. Set merged to false and write an escalation report covering converged_findings, the fixes attempted across all iterations, and the last test_output, for a human to take over.
